questions.question: When a solid is melting, the temperature remains constant even though thermal energy is being supplied. Which of the following explains this observation?
options: The energy is used to break the bonds between the particles
The solid is not absorbing any thermal energy
The solid molecules are moving faster
The solid is giving out thermal energy
questions.explanation:
As stated in Section A of the Review, energy supplied during a change of state is used to break intermolecular bonds rather than increasing kinetic energy (temperature). 

**Common mistake:** Thinking energy absorption stops during melting.

questions.question: Which statement about evaporation is correct?
options: Evaporation occurs at the boiling point
Evaporation occurs when a gas turns into a liquid
Evaporation occurs more slowly at higher temperatures
questions.explanation:
Evaporation involves the loss of high-energy particles, which reduces the average kinetic energy and temperature of the remaining liquid. 

**Common mistake:** Believing evaporation only happens at $100^\circ\text{C}$.

questions.question: The graph shows the temperature of a substance as it is cooled. In which section is the substance solidifying?nn[Graph shows: A-B (sloping down), B-C (horizontal at $60^\circ\text{C}$), C-D (sloping down)]
questions.explanation:
Solidification (freezing) occurs at a constant temperature, represented by the horizontal plateau (B to C). 

**Common mistake:** Selecting the sloping sections where temperature is changing.

questions.question: A student wants wet clothes on a washing line to dry as quickly as possible. Which set of conditions should she choose?
options: Cold, still air, and spread out
questions.explanation:
Evaporation rate increases with temperature, air movement (wind), and surface area (spread out). 

**Common mistake:** Thinking folding clothes (smaller area) will help them dry.

questions.question: Which row in the table correctly compares boiling and evaporation?nn| | Boiling | Evaporation |n| :--- | :--- | :--- |n| A | Throughout liquid | At surface only |n| B | At any temperature | At fixed temperature |n| C | No bubbles produced | Bubbles produced |n| D | Causes cooling | Temperature remains constant |
questions.explanation:
Boiling occurs throughout the liquid, whereas evaporation is a surface phenomenon. 

**Common mistake:** Swapping the temperature properties in Row B.
